title: pulicaria dysenterica subsp. dentata
intro: Pulicaria dysenterica subsp. dentata, also known as fleabane or bitter fleabane, is a perennial plant species native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its bitter and astringent compounds, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Pulicaria dysenterica subsp. dentata include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, making it a valuable herb in folk medicine. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tea or tincture, and its leaves and flowers can be used fresh or dried to extract its medicinal properties.
